topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The stark white paper lay spread across the kitchen table, the fluorescent light catching the precise lettering of the genetic report. My breath hitched, a sharp, involuntary intake of air that seemed too loud in the quiet morning. My fingers trembled as I traced the section detailing the familial matches. It wasn't a match; it was a confirmation. The results pointed to a biological link I hadn't known existed, a lineage that suddenly felt both monumental and utterly destabilizing. I pushed the paper away, the sound of the rustling sound amplified in the silence. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drumbeat demanding an explanation that wasn't forthcoming. I stared at the ceiling, trying to anchor myself to something solid, but the floor seemed to tilt beneath my feet.

[story 2]

When the courier handed over the sealed envelope, the weight of it felt like a physical burden pressing down on my chest. Inside, the DNA results were laid out, clinical and undeniable. I read the names, and a coldness washed over me, chilling me to the bone despite the warmth of the afternoon sun streaming through the window. I reread the comparison charts, my eyes skipping over the data until they snagged on the undeniable markers. A sudden, dizzying sensation swept through my limbs, making me sway slightly. I gripped the edge of the desk until my knuckles turned white, trying to steady the internal tremor that threatened to pull me under. I needed to speak to someone, to process this seismic shift in my understanding of my own history.

[story 3]

I sat on the edge of my bed, the printout clutched so tightly my nails dug crescent moons into the paper. Each word swam before my eyes, an incomprehensible tapestry of shared ancestry. A deep, hollow feeling settled in my stomach, a churning sensation that made my throat constrict. I kept glancing toward the closed door, half-expecting someone to walk in and shatter the fragile reality I was trying to construct. My hands began to shake uncontrollably, a fine vibration running up my arms. I swallowed hard, the dryness in my mouth making the action painful, and whispered a single, ragged question into the empty room, "What is this?"
